commit.message:
Grid IQ flow restructuring: unified pre-sale and post-sale flows

Restructures Grid IQ from disconnected tools into a guided two-stage flow
matching the product spec. Stage 1 (pre-sale) creates a multi-herd consignment
with per-category processor vs saleyard comparison. Stage 2 (post-sale) links
actual kill sheets with auto-matching, runs post-kill analysis, and confirms
sale with adjustable herd allocations.

New files:
- pre-sale-actions.ts: Multi-herd consignment + per-allocation analysis engine
- post-sale-actions.ts: Post-kill analysis + sale confirmation with head deduction
- pre-sale-flow.tsx: 3-step guided pre-sale component
- post-sale-flow.tsx: 3-step guided post-sale component with kill sheet auto-match
- category-breakdown.tsx: Per-category comparison table for multi-herd analyses
- prediction-accuracy.tsx: Pre-sale estimate vs actual outcome comparison
- consignments/[id]/post-sale/page.tsx: Post-sale flow entry page
- docs/GRID-IQ-FLOW-SPEC.md: Complete product flow specification

Modified files:
- analyse/page.tsx: Uses PreSaleFlow instead of AnalysisForm
- analysis/[id]/page.tsx: Category breakdown, prediction accuracy, consignment link
- consignments/[id]/page.tsx: Linked analyses, post-sale flow entry button
- consignments/actions.ts: Added updateConsignment for editable pending consignments
- page.tsx: Pending consignments section with action-needed highlighting

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: add README and VM deployment guide (#12)

- README.md: full project overview covering tech stack, project

- docs/deployment.md: step-by-step VM deployment guide for Ubuntu
  22.04 covering server setup, data artifact build, Uvicorn +
  Next.js services, Nginx reverse proxy with SSE config, systemd
  service units, health checks, update workflow, backups, and
  troubleshooting

commit.message:
fix(reflect): align prompts with finalized LLM Expert text

Update FRICTION_CLASSIFICATION_GUIDANCE and EFFECTIVE_PATTERN_CLASSIFICATION_GUIDANCE
to match the exact finalized text from LLM Expert review:

- Friction: opens with _reasoning mention, removes tooling-limitation precision block,
  uses "Step 1/2/3" labeling in decision tree, adds "Missing X caused Y" framing rule
- Patterns: adds "Trivial self-corrections" to baseline exclusions, uses 4-step
  "Step 1/2/3/4" driver decision tree with stronger collaborative constraint
- generateSessionAnalysisPrompt: adds _reasoning: (REQUIRED) inline field descriptions
  for friction_points and effective_patterns lists
- JSON examples updated to richer illustrative versions from LLM Expert

feat(npm): add npm package configuration for binary distribution

Add package.json, bin/cli.js wrapper, copy-binary script, and .npmignore
to support publishing the MCP server as an npm package. The CLI wrapper
finds the bundled binary and spawns it with inherited stdio for MCP
JSON-RPC communication.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Restore streaming LLM→TTS pipeline for better voice latency

The non-streaming workaround (commit 7e4762f) was a misdiagnosis —
the 1-token SSE responses were caused by missing max_tokens, not
broken streaming. With max_tokens=2047 now set, streaming works and
gives much better time-to-first-audio: sentence detection feeds TTS
while the LLM is still generating.

commit.message:
Complete sanitization coverage, atomic generation counting, ntfy routing

- SEC-007: Sanitize article fields (title, source, summary, description) before
  LLM prompt injection in generate_content, generate_newsletter, search_score.
- SEC-008: Sanitize stored feedback in _enrich_prompt_with_feedback before LLM.
- BUG-010: Fix error message for Free plan — show "generazioni totali" instead
  of "generazioni/mese" when user hits lifetime limit.
- BUG-011: Atomic generation counting via PostgreSQL RPC function
  (increment_generation_count) to prevent race conditions. Falls back to
  read-modify-write if RPC not available.
- BUG-012: Fix ntfy topic routing — _do_fetch and test_ntfy now use per-user
  ntfy_topic from profile instead of global NTFY_TOPIC.
- BUG-013: Add field allowlist to db.update_schedule() to prevent arbitrary
  column overwrites.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(reflect): actor-neutral friction/pattern prompts + CoT _reasoning field

Fixes AI-centric bias in session analysis prompts. Three changes:

1. FRICTION_CLASSIFICATION_GUIDANCE: rewrite all 9 category definitions
   to be actor-neutral (no "AI pursued..." framing) and replace the
   linear attribution logic with an explicit 3-step CoT decision tree.
   LLM must write reasoning in _reasoning before assigning attribution.

2. EFFECTIVE_PATTERN_CLASSIFICATION_GUIDANCE: add baseline exclusion
   (routine pre-edit reads are NOT context-gathering), user infrastructure
   recognition (CLAUDE.md/agent configs/skills → user-driven), and a
   4-step driver decision tree that checks user infra first.

3. _reasoning CoT scratchpad field: added as the FIRST key in friction
   and pattern JSON schema examples (forces left-to-right reasoning before
   classification). Added _reasoning?: string to AnalysisResponse interface.
   Saved to DB automatically. [cot-monitor] warnings in parseAnalysisResponse
   flag models that skip the CoT step.

Closes #137

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Set max_tokens=2047 default in VLM requests to fix 1-token responses

axllm defaults to very few output tokens when max_tokens is not
specified in the request body. Set it to the model's full context
window (2047 from AXERA-TECH config). axllm will stop at KV cache
capacity regardless.

commit.message:
Configurable severity-to-priority mapping for ntfy channels

Allow per-channel customization of how event severities map to ntfy
priorities when using auto mode. The mapping is stored in the channel
JSONB config and editable via the settings UI.
New defaults: info->default, warning->high, critical->high (not urgent).

feat: add server resource metrics to status page

- heartbeat.py now collects CPU, memory, disk, network via psutil each minute
- New /api/metrics endpoint returns live system stats (30s cache)
- /api/uptime response now includes metrics history for sparklines
- status.html: metric cards with gauge bars (CPU, memory, swap, disk,
  network RX/TX, server uptime) + sparkline charts for CPU, memory,
  network RX/TX over last 24h
- UptimeRobot API key configured — both monitors active

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add multi-modal input, thought_signature round-trips, model fallback chain, and retry logic

- Multi-modal input: convert OpenAI content arrays (text + base64 images) to
  Gemini inlineData parts so all models can accept image input
- Thought signature preservation: pass thought_signature through on tool_calls
  in responses so clients can echo it back for Gemini 3.x round-trips, with
  server-side cache as fallback
- Auto-downgrade to 2.5 model when tool round-trip lacks thought_signature
- Model fallback chain with per-model cooldown timers: 3.1-pro → 3-pro →
  2.5-pro → 2.5-flash → 2.5-flash-lite, using reset times from 429 errors
- Fail fast with 429 when all models exhausted instead of blocking on retries
- Fix thinkingConfig when falling back across Gemini 3/2.5 boundary
  (thinkingLevel vs thinkingBudget)
- Resolve tool result function names from preceding assistant tool_calls
- Add user_prompt_id, topP, topK to match upstream Gemini CLI config
- Raise body size limit to 100MB for large context payloads
- Remove non-existent gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview from model list
- Fix maxOutputTokens from 65536 to 65535 (exclusive upper bound)

commit.message:
feat: extended detail when using --section

Using --section now automatically shows deep-dive information instead
of the standard overview. Each section gets platform-specific data
sourced from WMI/PowerShell (Windows) and system_profiler/sysctl/ioreg
(macOS):

- gpu: real-time usage/VRAM/temp/clocks (nvidia-smi), driver date,
  DirectX version, connected display resolutions (WMI)
- cpu: cache sizes (L1/L2/L3), virtualization support, power plan,
  microarchitecture codename, per-core usage bar chart
- memory: DIMM slot inventory with type/speed/manufacturer/part number,
  slot count, max supported capacity (WMI Win32_PhysicalMemory)
- disk: SMART health status, physical drive model/serial/firmware/
  interface type (NVMe/SATA/USB), partition table
- network: Wi-Fi SSID/signal/band/channel/security, per-interface
  gateway/DNS/DHCP lease info (ipconfig /all + netsh)
- battery: design vs full-charge capacity, wear %, chemistry,
  charge/discharge rate (WMI root\wmi + ioreg on Mac)
- system: BIOS version/date, motherboard vendor/model, Windows
  edition/build, chassis type, Secure Boot, TPM status

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Shorten system prompt to prevent Qwen3 thinking mode activation

Complex/long system prompts trigger Qwen3-VL-2B's thinking mode,
which consumes the entire 2,047 token budget on <think> tags,
leaving only 1 token for the actual response. Confirmed via curl
testing on Pi hardware.

Fix disk % chart to report max filesystem usage across all mounts

Heartbeat was only reporting root / usage, which is nearly empty on
NAS devices where data lives on separate mounts. Now loops over all
physical, non-network partitions and reports the maximum usage -- matching
the filter logic already used by the full telemetry disk collector.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: hybrid commentary system for --diagnose

Adds plain-English analysis to every diagnostic finding:
- Static knowledge base covers 38 common Windows Event IDs with
  explanations, concern levels (ignore/monitor/investigate/fix_now),
  and actionable advice — works fully offline, zero API calls
- Claude API (Haiku) handles unrecognized events in a single batched
  call; falls back gracefully with a clear message when key is absent
- Heuristic findings (disk full, RAM pressure, etc.) always use static
  commentary since their meaning is known at collection time
- Concern badge shown inline in event tables; actionable findings
  (investigate/fix_now) surface a dedicated Action Items panel below
  each category with explanation + specific next step
- Set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY or use --api-key for AI-powered analysis

commit.message:
Replace metronome clicks with melodies for skip counting

Easy uses Twinkle Twinkle intervals, medium uses Row Row Row Your Boat,
hard uses ascending scale patterns. Each beat plays a musical note
instead of a click, making skip counting feel like a song — the way
kids actually learn it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Easy starts on multiples, medium/hard starts anywhere for deeper learning

Easy mode: 5, 10, 15 (memorize the pattern through rhythm)
Medium/Hard: 8, 13, 18 (forces understanding addition, not recitation)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix skip counting to start on multiples (5, 10, 15 not 8, 13, 18)

Skip counting sequences now always start from a multiple of the step
value, matching how kids learn: count by 5s = 5, 10, 15, 20...
Fixed in RhythmCountGame, RhythmicCountingDrill, and SequencingDrill.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add SMS frequency selector, custom message, and clear local data on logout

Users can choose daily, weekly, or monthly reminder frequency.
Optional custom message replaces boilerplate in SMS reminders.
Logout now clears localStorage anonymous data to prevent merge prompt.

commit.message:
Add 5 training drills and 3 brain games for deeper cognitive coverage

New drills: Rhythmic Counting (skip counting to a beat), Mental Math
Strategy (make-10, doubles, compensation), Fact Family (inverse
operations with triangle visual), Everyday Math (tipping, change,
recipes), Number Decomposition (base-10 blocks, flexible decomposition).

New games: Rhythm Count (game version with scoring), Number Balance
(visual balance scale, solve for unknowns), What's My Rule (find
hidden input→output pattern rules).

Total: 18 training drills across 6 domains, 12 brain games.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

  single column at 480px for small phones

  offset across all interactive elements

Bump version 0.1.10 → 0.1.11

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

balance: 暂时禁用所有时间类技能/遗物/附魔

- ClassResourceFilter: time 加入 DISABLED_RESOURCES，
  所有 time 相关产出者/转化器/增幅器/连接器从技能池移除
- relicPicker: time_thief/doomsday/perfect_rhythm 加入 DISABLED_RELICS
- enchantments: ench_trans_time 加入 DISABLED_ENCHANTMENTS
恢复时只需移除对应 Set 中的条目

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Switch LLM from streaming to non-streaming infer (axllm SSE workaround)

axllm's SSE streaming produces truncated 1-char responses — the model
generates <think> tags that consume the token budget, and streaming
cleanup issues truncate the output. Non-streaming infer() works reliably
(verified by direct curl testing). TTS is still done sentence-by-sentence
for natural playback pacing.

We can revisit streaming once we understand axllm's SSE behavior better.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Per-user prompts, guided tour, in-app notifications

FASE 1 — Per-user prompt architecture:
- New user_prompts table in Supabase for per-user prompt storage
- Replace global Python variables with DB-backed per-user prompts
- Lazy initialization: base prompts copied on first generation
- _get_prompt() loads from DB with fallback to BASE_PROMPTS
- enrich_prompt() now persists enriched prompts per-user in DB
- Generic prompts: removed hardcoded "Juan" references

FASE 2 — Interactive guided tour:
- Replace generic 3-slide onboarding overlay with spotlight tour
- 5 steps highlighting real UI elements (source tabs, opinion,
  platform selector, generate button, libreria tab)
- Spotlight technique with box-shadow overlay
- Glassmorphism tooltip with step counter and navigation
- Responsive: fixed bottom tooltip on mobile

FASE 3 — In-app notification system:
- New notifications table with RLS policies
- Bell icon 🔔 in topbar with red badge counter
- Dropdown panel with notification list and mark-all-read
- 3 API endpoints: GET list, POST read, POST read-all
- Auto-triggers on content generation and prompt enrichment
- 60-second polling for real-time updates
- 9 new db.py functions for prompts + notifications

Add list-credit-notes action, party_name in list-payments, router mapping

commit.message:
Improve virality, SEO and visual quality

Visual:
- Fix on-screen title: keep zodiac glyphs (♈…) strip only 4-byte emoji
  that Montserrat can't render — titles were showing stripped of symbols
- Title now persists full video duration instead of disappearing at 3s
- Sign label: full name + emoji centered at top (e.g. "♈ ARIES")
  replacing the weak 3-letter abbreviation at 75% opacity
- Caption position moved 82% → 70% to avoid YouTube UI button overlap

Audio:
- Voice model: eleven_turbo_v2 → eleven_multilingual_v2 (more expressive)
- Music volume: 0.12 → 0.18 (better mystical atmosphere)

Content / SEO:
- Script CTA now ends with loop-bait teaser to drive replays and follows
- Title prompt allows 80 chars (was 60) for better search coverage
- YouTube description expanded: channel boilerplate + content type label
  + fixed hashtags appended (#astrology #zodiac #horoscope #shorts)

Scheduling:
- Pipeline cron: 17:00 → 22:00 UTC (6pm EDT, peak astrology audience)
- post_time config updated to 18:00 EST

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
